mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in CPIAUCSL reflect accelerating inflation that compresses real corporate earnings and elevates equity risk premiums, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 50 percent of months when CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month during any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals labor market slack that weakens consumer spending growth and corporate revenue expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between the federal funds rate level and the industrial production index level captures the joint stress of monetary restrictiveness and real output; elevated FEDFUNDS combined with subdued INDPRO tightens financial conditions and produces negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The bivariate interaction of FEDFUNDS and INDPRO yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant in a 24-month panel regression.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) predicts positive S&P 500 returns during late-cycle expansions because consumer demand is the marginal driver of profits, while the signal is inert during contractions.
failure_condition: During late-cycle expansions, month-over-month RSAFS growth fails to positively predict 20-day S&P 500 returns with a Rank IC above 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in industrial production (INDPRO month-over-month change below zero) reveals supply-side weakness and inventory destocking that prompts negative earnings revisions, leading to negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of months when INDPRO contracts over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in housing starts (HOUST) lead the business cycle through reduced construction activity and credit contraction; month-over-month decreases predict negative 60-day S&P 500 returns via lower materials demand and employment.
failure_condition: HOUST month-over-month declines do not coincide with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55 percent of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.0 percent sustains restrictive monetary conditions that raise corporate borrowing costs and equity risk premiums, causing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns until policy eases.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0 percent, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of triggered months over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Falling nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) concurrent with restrictive Fed policy (FEDFUNDS above 4.0 percent) creates dual pressure on corporate margins and discount rates, producing negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition of falling PAYEMS and FEDFUNDS above 4.0 percent fails to produce negative 60-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in logistic regression over a 3-year window.
